reviews.review_title: Love the graphics but there were missed
reviews.review_description:
Opportunities.

Love the graphics and the story line, but the game is a little glitchy.  At times you can figure out the next steps and there were times where I did not have a clue.  There's alot of reading and standing around. Other times alot of back and forth. Even with all that, I like the game and will play it again.   My suggestion when you remaster this game make it more interactive.  For example when climbing a latter or opening a door, instead of using A button, use your hands.  Another is, use the B button to show what direction to go for the next puzzle.  Something like a trail of leaves, butterflies and snow showing what direction to go. These elements will match you scenes.  Over its not the best but the graphics and story line were great.

Graphically, this game is amazing. I've been playing it for a couple of hours now and I'm getting increasingly frustrated by features/bugs that simply should not exist in a game at this price point.
The movement is sooo slow. Even in teleport mode you seem to be only able to jump small distances. It just takes sooo long to get around. 
The hand tracking is frequently horrible. Your VR hands often seem to just stick and refuse to follow your hand/controller position. Trying to pick an object from the backpack is horrible. Overall, a disappointing experience so far.
UPDATE: Game now completed and, I have to say that despite the many flaws, I quite enjoyed it. Some puzzles were incredibly easy (the bandstand - despite spending far too long getting to the grapes) but some puzzles would have remained Illusive to me without a Walkthrough cheat sheet (I've still no idea how I was supposed to create the cocktail???). The ending comes at you very quickly and inexplicably. I sort of expected to get to Syberia...perhaps that's just me.

OK I've finished the game and recommend it highly. Very entertaining and keeps you interested. There is still some jankiness in the game, and required a couple of restarts. There are some issues the developers definitely need to fix, and I'm sure they will patch them, but any fan of puzzle style games in interesting settings will love it.

I get that this app had a lot of initially negative reviews, maybe it was released a bit too early before some of the bugs were worked out. But I believe it's had a patch since then, and I can say that even despite any lingering issues, it's a delightful walk down memory lane. As someone who immensely enjoyed the original Syberia games on PC, this is delightful. The immersive atmosphere is such a great addition to what is otherwise some adept storytelling and clever puzzles. Please don't hesitate to give this a chance, even despite the negative reviews. It's a lovely enhancement to what was already a great game from a decade ago.

Yes, there are some issues. Not enough to keep from enjoying the game. Just a little patience, and you can enjoy the story, the beautiful stylization, and the really neat mechanics. I love the artwork and the way the long journey portions are shown. I really loved it and look forward to another adventure.

I just finished the game, it even made me cry at the very end. I loved every second of it. The story was great, the characters, and the humour also. Quite an adventure. It's no oil painting but a lovely watercolour where you can immerse yourself and find that nostalgia you didn't know you had ☺️

This game has plenty of bugs: the music cuts out, dialogues break off, objects float in the air, and the heroine constantly gets stuck on empty spots. But! This is that same amazing Syberia I played back in my childhood. And it looks wonderful in first person. You can simply walk around and enjoy the atmosphere created by a brilliant artist.

reviews.review_title: A gem! So fun to revisit in VR!
reviews.review_description: What a fun trip down memory lane, but completely refreshed! Only about an hour or so in, but it’s bringing me so much joy to be INSIDE Syberia after having loved the original for so many years. I think the initial release of the VR version was buggy-hence the ratings, but I absolutely adore what I’ve experienced so far! Sure there could be a few optimization improvements, but the game is completely playable and enjoyable from what I’ve seen. Very immersive and the initial puzzles have been really fun. Love that I can turn off the subtitles and screen reader to really immerse myself. Also love the little interactive details like the cellphone and objects that you can fool around with. Notebook is a bit laggy when up, and I wish the environments didn’t need to render by player distance, but that’s just a minor nitpick. For what they’ve packed into the game it’s totally understandable that some compromises were made to make it functional. Overall, I feel I’ve been transported inside one of my favorite adventure games, and that alone is worth 5 stars! Hope we get to see Syberia II ported to VR in the future!

Thank you to the developers for this VR port. I’m really enjoying it; I know it has its flaws, and the graphics could be a bit better, but in my opinion, it’s a truly great game. I definitely recommend it.

Moving between locations is so tedious! The long meaningless conversations are also tedious. I have never played this game on any other platform, so I have no nostalgia associated with it. It’s glitchy also. The graphics are good.

PROS: Lots of story, fun puzzles, areas to explore, different reactions based on your choices, pretty

CONS: Vocal glitches for a narrative based game, some instructions are wrong (second is third etc), Sometimes your grab actions don’t respond and that’s bad bc your grab actions are necessary to move a scent forward. In scenes with multiple characters the wrong character moves their mouth. So many loading screens. Just give us a fast travel map. I don’t really know how the story ended bc at the last scene my notebook got stuck up.

Game is miles better than launch, yet not free from issues

Movement - 

Happy to report full smooth movement has been patched in with a speed slider for walk/run/turn.  All of which are now fast.  Smooth turning can cause massive FPS issues, so I would recommend turning irl as much as possible, but also not necessary.

Subtitles - 

Now an option to completely shut of voice subtitles, as well as reading overlay text. All of which are a fantastic addition 

Audio - 

Basically sounds like its all the original audio from the game.  Absolutely zero spatial audio and feels very mono.  It's not great, but passable.  Spatial audio is very much needed

Graphics - 

Mixed bag.  Some things look great while some assets are low poly and/or 2d.  Overall, its pretty good for what this game is/was. It's mobile hardware after all.

Performance - 

Still has some issues.  While Im not too far into the game, since I refused to play until it was patched, a lot of my issues stemmed from smooth turning.  There still are a lot of stuttering here and there, but they have improved a lot. Still a mixed bag but def playable

Overall -

The low reviews this game has were 100% warranted. I wanted to quickly make this review for anyone currently interested in where its at.  Based on my first impressions, I would give this a 4/5.  If something happens the further I get, I will update the review.  They have fixed a large majority of the issues here.  It still needs further improvements, but if you're a fan of the original titles back in the day like me, I think you'll be happy.  I suggest using QGO and turning I'll to help the performance even more

This game was visually stunning! I loved all the environments and scenes. The puzzles were very easy and felt more like a search and find game. The story was good but was way too much talking for my liking. The only reason I didn’t give it 5 stars was because of the few bugs it has. It didn’t make the game unplayable for me, but I definitely felt frustrated at times. One scene completely glitched and got skipped over and I had to watch the scene on YouTube because I had no clue what the character had asked me to do for them. I didn’t have any issues with movement like other comments mentioned. I guess that’s just a personal preference thing. Overall this game brought me a lot of enjoyment. It was worth the money and I recommend it!

Terribly done remaster on VR. 1 out of 10. A freebie. I got to the point where the train took me away and the valadilenes, even though there was no mammoth on board, is this what you call a finished product? It's just a slap in the face to fans at a cost of 30 dollars.

I got my key from DC GA.
I've played the original PC version of this game more than 10 years ago. I wanted to play it because it's referred to as a masterpiece story, but I had to give up after about 2 hour playing because of the slow moving to places and too slow scrolling of dialog speeches.
According to the reviews, this VR version seems to have inherited the original version of the frustration at first. From what I have started and experienced, I think I will be able to enjoy the game till the end by supporting high-speed movement and conversation skipping. Thanks to Dev.
However, what I want is that given the story adventure game that only supports Quest 3 and does not require a quick response, I want to the graphics resolution increased more. And that there is no Korean among many supported languages.
If you need Korean translation volunteers, I can be one of them.

===
I completed all this and recommend. Please make the other series as VR.

reviews.review_title: Update fixed it: move speed, smooth turn
reviews.review_description: Loved the original on PC since it was new, I was looking forward to this. Beautifully realized graphically, no complaints there. On the initial release I had some severe issues that the first patch has thankfully fixed with options menu settings. I encountered an issue where the boy I was supposed to follow vanished entirely, but I was able to proceed by looking up reminders of where he would have led me, and I suspect that issue could relate to how in my save game he was stuck outside the attic before the patch. I hope some other people who had an initially rocky experience will give the patched version a try and update their reviews to reflect it's current state.

reviews.review_title: Script heavy game but gameplay is odd
reviews.review_description: It’s interesting but a bit strange that you have to click a to initial talk with a NPC, and then also have to choose a subject from a notepad to discuss about. Arguably unique way of interacting but just not my cup of tea. Also, how is a game in 2025 only have “snap turn” but no “smooth turn”? The constant black when turning is really taking away from the immersion. Additionally, I can’t tell if it’s me or the game, but the pop up click A to chat with NPC window pops up randomly, even when not around an npc or while in the middle of an interaction. Causing I to restart the conversation. Furthermore, the walking movement is strange. Very slow and I “hear” 3 steps for every one step. Lastly, the graphics so far has been disappointing and not what I was expecting for the price. Hopefully just means you get a substantial amount of gameplay for the price. So far, nothing has popped out to me to say “wow” but still too early in the game. So hopefully I’m proven wrong.

I was so excited for this and now I'm praying for a refund. Looks great. Beautiful environments. But... as many reviewers have said, I hate the dialog in front of me. Can't turn it off. Can't see the NPC through the text. And most of all and the primary reason I don't like this game is because she's snail paced slow. It would be 50 hrs long if I didn't attempt to run. I prefer to move quickly as most do. But then my thumb starts hurting to the point I cannot play anymore. We need autorun!!!  And don't get me started on the snap turn vignette, or blink or whatever it is. It's awful. If the devs improve these things, I'll change my review. If I am permitted a refund, and the devs fix it, I will repurchase. For now, it's a definite no for me which is disappointing because I was excited to see this game.

At original release this game was unplayable. For multiple reasons (see the many other reviews) I refunded the game after an hour.
  I noticed the announcement for the first patch and decided to give it another try. It is actually quite enjoyable now. You can tell it’s a port of a fairly old flat game, due to some of the game mechanics/ interactions, but it hasn’t affected my enjoyment of the game. I LOVE puzzle games and this one has kept me well entertained.

Walking speed is adjustable and running is solved now, the text windows can be switched off, and slightly better graphics now. Smooth turning option added but it isn't "so smooth". What I'm still missing most is to remove annoying blinking during step turning. I'm adding some stars and will add another if the patches will continue to bring this magnificent game into the shape it deserves.

I was excited to dive into Syberia in virtual reality, eager to explore a richly crafted from the ground-up for VR. I had high hopes of immersing myself in the industrial landscape of Syberia, reliving the nostalgia of a puzzle game that holds a special place in my heart from my past. With the launch of Patch 1.0.058, many of my initial concerns about the game have been addressed, allowing me to dive deeper into its world. The addition of various locomotion options means it’s no longer restricted to just teleportation and snap turning for the best experience, and the significant enhancements in anti-aliasing have nearly eliminated the harsh jagged edges. However, despite these improvements, there are still some visual aspects that can disrupt immersion, such as detail pop-in and subtitles that occasionally obscure the faces of characters you interact with. Besides these issues, and the sporadically clumsy controls, the puzzle elements stay faithful to the original game, with some modifications to enrich the VR experience. The overall puzzle design is captivating and fun to tackle. If you have fond memories of the game, you won’t be disappointed with its current offerings, as the major problems have been resolved. I just hope we receive a few more updates to tackle issues like pop-in, frame drops, and perhaps adjust the intensity of the subtitles for those who rely on them.

This review has been revised after the release of Patch 1.0.058 on December 6th. (Original score 2 stars)

reviews.review_title: Not Perfect but well worth a go !
reviews.review_description: I have been playing this for a few days (on and off) and after a few attempts have completed the first part . There are a lot of negative reviews on this game but having never played it before on any system I really enjoy playing this in VR . Yes it has a few little issues but I don’t find them as bad as what many others are saying . I would give it 5 Stars for its ability to ‘take you to another place’ but it’s not quite there yet . Having said that I do recommend this game to others .

Unplayable mess. Events fail to trigger, movement is too slow, even the notebook that is supposed to guide you is all messed up. I am only on chapter 2 and cannot progress any further. Avoid at all costs until it is patched. We pay hard earned money for these games and they should work. I don’t think this game was ever playtested.

developer_response.body: Hi, we heard all feedback and are cooking a patch to improve and solve many issues you are talking about notably related to the resolution but also to have smooth turns. We will give the possibility to tweak the speed of movements with sliders in the option menu. We really care about your feedback and the patch will come very soon.

reviews.review_description:
It is a great game if you get it when it is on sale. Me and my mom went half and half and we’re gonna share the game.  I think currently at the stage it is, it’s only worth about 15 bucks. If they make you run faster and smoother and they improved the graphics. The graphics are OK but not $30 worth of graphics. The real difficulty I have in the game is two go between areas, you have to click the A button and it takes forever to load-(10 seconds, which doesn’t seem like a lot but when you have to go across the map and you have to do that six times in a row just to get to another area, that’s an entire minute just spent loading!) So if there was a way for them to keep a couple maps loaded in the background, it would be so much better. ￼For example, to enter the locomotive, you have to press the button. You can’t just walk in the door. This annoyance￼ also eliminates a lot of the fun of the game because there’s no opening doors to go into a new area It’s just pressing the A button and loading. It really needs some improvement even if they just made a long passage way to give the game time to load like what gorilla tag does, that would still be better. The game would almost be worth $30.
Hope this review helps and I do think the game has promise!

Looking at the other reviews, there were several that didn't rate it very highly. I'm not sure why. I never played any other version of the game, either PC or other. 
I THOROUGHLY enjoyed Syberia. It kept me engaged for weeks. I played a little every few days. I had some issues at the beginning with the Momo character locking up and I had to start the game over from the beginning. But after that, I didn't have that issue any longer. 
The game was so engaging, well acted, interesting, not too hard and not too easy. I loved it and I am looking forward to other games like this. I'm wondering if there is a sequel to this game. 
Thanks for a great job on this! I highly recommend it.

Yes the game has some minor issues like running speed and having to hold the joystick to run, bit these are things i imagine the devs will address with a patch. The game itself i personally think looks good on q3. Its not red matter or ghost town, but i wouldnt say its horrible like some of the other reviews. I grew up with games like this and love the fact that i can actually walk around and interact within these worlds. Heres hoping the devs are listening and will address some of the concerns.

reviews.review_title: terrific game in very decent port
reviews.review_description:
I don't think I need to comment on this classic, it's a great game that has passed the test of time. But seeing all 1, 2 star ratings here I was supposing the port had gone terribly wrong and worthless. Of course, that's not the case, it's just the usual shill army annoyed at another exclusive Quest port of a major game. I played up the cave, experienced very few of the fake issues here and I just know Quest limitations - the port is as good and legit as it gets, much like equally excellent Riven or The 7th Guest. These are among the best games you can find in a device overflowing with absolute shovelware. 

I have 2 requests for the devs tho: 1) make run speed the standard walk speed and get rid of "click and hold" to run, just use click or get rid of run altogether; and 2) have an option to turn off soft glow around interactive objects for extra challenge and make them glow only when close - it's not great looking at them from distances...

I played after the most recent update and faced no game breaking bugs at all well done on the update! Absolutely enjoyable.


I have never played the original Syberia but I have had my fair share of point and click adventures. This VR port truly captures the slow pace of such games and adds to it the VR interaction in a meaningful way. Kudus to the team.

I played the original on pc. I was so excited for this to come to vr. But I have played down to the last parts of the game and it won’t let me finish it. I don’t see an option to re start the different chapters only the current chapter you are on. If the game stops working, you would have to restart game from the beginning! Not to mention all the other irritating problems with the movement of the game! I guess I won’t be finishing this because I’m not restarting the whole game to go through this again!

I played the original so I was very hyped for this game and have not been disappointed. No glitches as far as I know yet and the gameplay has been very smooth. Thank you so much microids team you’ve literally made my dreams come true with this game I’m so happy and I only hope you’ll adapt the rest of the games in VR 🙏 I just want to live in this world forever please and thank you so much! My absolute favorite VR game! The best!

Really bad graphic. Just horrible view of piece of paper as example. No view from window. So basically it is a poor vr from a great game. 30 usd to a trash.
UPD: change from 3 stars to 1. Game just broken. Too many bugs. Also meta rejected my refund( Don't buy this trash

The intro menu area is a train carriage running at an awful frame rate. Looking out of the window shows trees stuttering past as double images. Going into the first area of the game, a hotel, has a meeting with an unblinking character (the hotel owner) who straight-up disappears when he’s led you to your room. Went back to the lobby where he’d apparently teleported to, and asked him all available questions. Said I was going out to look at the rest of the town but there’s no way of opening the hotel door, or clicking to exit, or anything.

Seriously considering refunding. Also, performance is poor on Quest 3. Lots of stutters and hitches.

One of my favorite PC games. Love it and its sequels. 

Update: they released a patch the helped with them movement and increased the pace of running. 

I am now enjoying the game more. Seems like the graphics got a little better as well

reviews.review_title: Def a must buy for syberia lovers
reviews.review_description:
I have very small gripes hopefully a patch addresses  these issues first is what others say the snap turning the second is the painful running like let's have an auto run toggle or something and the third is a bug I have found and that's sometimes conversations  skip mid flow 
Other than small bit of polish for a first stab microids once again you proven why I love your games and why this is a must have for any syberia fan the factory alone blew me away how big it is

I have been looking forward to this game for a while. I never played the original but the trailers made this look like it was going to be a bit of a banger. But as it goes its just a clanger. So where do i start.
From the off the movement is very frustrating. I mean this has to be the slowest walk in history, and pressing forward on the controller is very uncomfortable, and is not good for the controller.

The mechanics are very clunky and placing items down and picking up could be a lot better eg laying the train permit on the receptionist area of the notarys office looks and feels very unatual.

When talking with oscar after making his legs and again on the train his conversation suddenly finishes.

I placed the staff punch card into the machine and had to go back again to speak to Osacar and ask him for it again before the console started to work.

Can the devs please contact me to let me know how i can get in as this is very frustrating . All in all i have had a very disappointing experience with this game so far and considering how much it cost I feel a bit ripped off. It seems to me that this  game is still in beta mode at the best and should have never came out because it's far from ready.

****UPDATE****

Since the patch has been applied walking and running is a 100 times but that's about it. There is still major issues with the whole smoothness of the game. Eg. when in Barrockstadt placing the blocks in the band stand holes feels very unnatural and awkward, grabbing of the egg is again very awkward, when speaking to npc's sometimes they never finish what they are saying and it gets cut off and you seem to walk on water on the canal at times. Where do I stop? This continues to be a big disappointment and I wish I hadn't wasted my money on this trash. VERY POOR EFFORT

Why I cannot turn of this anoying blink while turning?? Even if blink is set at zero? Also dialogs interrupt for no reason. This game was badly tested if at all.
I play  only because I love genre.

I wanted to like this game so badly because I loved the PC game, but this is unplayable. The navigation is horrendous, the dialog boxes are annoying and always in the way, your hands can’t interact with anything properly like opening a door, etc, and it’s glitchy. 

The puzzles are redundant and boring. Actually, they’re not really puzzles, they’re more like looking for items that glow and placing an item there. If they thought this game was ready to go then they clearly don’t care about their customers. 

I kept trying to get past the annoying issues, but now the game seems hopelessly stuck. After leaving the attic, the boy I was supposed to follow seems to be stuck with his feet in the rocks and he won’t move. I can’t progress further in the game despite restarting several times.

I would like a refund please.

Updating my review it's offensive at one point they call one of the characters a re---d! ￼I had really high hopes and pre-ordered. The first thing that is the most bothersome is things in the distance like the Baker, where the flowers in the window boxes all have like flashing /squiggly outline, which, after a few minutes started to bother my eyes. Movement needs to be improved the step turn and I feel like the player should be able to pick the speed That they want to walk because it's so slow.

reviews.review_title: Another point of view to the original
reviews.review_description: I had problem in chapter 2 where the rector does not give the money although the bandstand is repaired. After the patch and replay the chapter 2, I can continue the game and just finished it. The graphic cannot be compared to Red Matter 2 or Asgard Wrath 2. However I enjoy the game and experience it again from 1st person point of view.

It’s extremely disappointing when a game glitches etc. To not even be able to finish the game is not good enough. Be warned people!!  

- I’m trying to make a certain cocktail for a certain lady in a wheelchair. She is stuck in the door way of the pier with James . He doesn’t bring her to the bar like I see in YouTube videos, she repeats same message over and over with no option to use notepad. . I am unable to do anything with the honey and hot tub as the handle will not turn. The lemon doesn’t get squeezed. The bar on switch does nothing. Bell in front desk cannot be pressed. Aircraft cannot return to previous area. I’ve reloaded numerous times and everything I see online doesn’t help for vr version. Uninstalled and reinstalled  still nothing. Not happy, can’t even refund as it’s played more than 2hrs. 🤬

I have played every version of this game for years, and my initial thoughts are just that-initial! 

Updated Comment as of 11.21 cannot play or enjoy at all movement mechanics are terrible and the god-aweful text box is annoying! Still praying for major update!!

Previous Comments: I would say 3.5 stars but not an option here, I will update the score after I play for a little while longer, but the one thing that stands out and is a deal breaker for me is the text box, which is eye level and follows your sight line. I want to see the characters and interact naturally with them. I have not found a way to shut them off, Devs PLEASE tell me how to shut that off, or give us an update to remove them. They just ruin the whole esthetic of the game 

If that can be fix, or if you could take a min. to tell me how to shut them off would gladly update my post and score.

 Thank you in advance!!
I've enjoyed this series for many years. I love witnessing the world, story and adventure in VR. A dream come true. Please tell me Syberia 2 will be coming to VR. It's my favorite in the series.

reviews.review_title: MASTERY OF ART AND TECHNIQUE!  TOP!
reviews.review_description:
A POETIC AND EMOTIONAL NARRATIVE
Syberia VR offers a profoundly contemplative experience: by embodying Kate Walker, you explore iconic settings from Sokal's universe, now in immersive 3D.
The automatons, steampunk machines, and alpine landscapes are faithfully recreated, and walking through these places strengthens the emotional connection with the story.
You move freely, manipulate levers, and solve puzzles with your hands, like in a VR point-and-click adventure.
The interaction feels very natural, offering true immersion in magnificent landscapes.
A NOSTALGIC WORK REVISITED WITH ELEGANCE AND DEPTH 
The soul of Syberia remains intact: the narrative is melancholic, full of dreams and memories.
Voice and text are in French for complete immersion.
You feel Kate's loneliness, but also her determination.
The transition to VR amplifies the intensity of key moments: conversations, discoveries, and contemplation become more real than ever.
Developers Microids and Virtuallyz clearly aimed to create an experience that is both faithful and innovative, respecting B. Sokal's work while fully leveraging the potential of VR.
If you enjoy contemplative adventures and powerful storytelling, this VR experience could very well be one of your favorites!

reviews.review_title: ❄️ Gentle Intro to the Syberia Universe
reviews.review_description:
I’d never played any game in the Syberia series before, and I was pleasantly surprised by how quickly I got absorbed into the story. Meeting the characters, discovering the world, and watching the narrative slowly unfold is genuinely captivating.

The puzzles are well-paced, the atmosphere is rich, and the combination of graphics, music, and overall mood is wonderfully executed. Smooth turning would enhance immersion even more, but I’m sure it’s something the studio will polish in future updates.

Looking forward to diving deeper into Syberia! ❄️

reviews.review_title: Like the story mode, need some polish
reviews.review_description:
Just finished the game. It took me about 12hours. 
I like the story mode. 
As this game is just released, there are minor glitches. If those are solved, I can give the game 5⭐️.
Those glitches doesn’t infect the story.

If you get stuck, you can watch my gameplay on my YT channel.

https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLRFURfvL_iloQlHF7UvXhF28J4-kN-Yba&si=efAaJ7dAJKPRPF-D

reviews.review_title: Seems like a fun and interesting game bu
reviews.review_description:
I have never played this game before and because it looked good I did pre order it. I don't mind the graphics but

Snap turn does suck luckily I can just turn my body but not everyone can

-the walking and running are ridiculous, you walk to slow and to hold the thumb stick is terrible so I had to use telephoto which I am not a fan of

Last is the talking windows there needs an option to move them so they are not in the way. 

For now I can't play the game with those 3 things above

Update 2: I see the devs are stating to address some of these issues...good to hear a patch is on the way! Update: I thought I'd get QGO and see if that makes a difference...can't say I can see much difference.
Original review: Played all the Syberia series on PC and loved it! Thoughts on Quest so far...I can't believe no one has mentioned how bad it is graphically? Really blurry, needs a graphic update. Other than that it's seems good.

davidj.340017 push down on the joystick and walking is quicker!
Enjoying the story and pace but snap turn 😞 it's killing me...pls give people smooth it's the best way to play vr
I enjoyed the original 'flat" title back then. If you like P&C adventures you should give it a try!
Walking and even running goes waaaaaaayy to slow, takes forever to walk from one place to the next, also very laggy with looking around
All issues are fixed. I can finally enjoy the game. Sad it was released too quickly harming the initial reviews. Greed knows no limits.

Played the game all the way through and got stuck 5 minutes from the end at alabaster station where I was trying to get to Oscar and was unable to walk over to Oscar to wind up the train I’ve even tried reloading the game to no avail
this is what is wrong with rushing games in todays era. unpolished, buggy, glitchy joke of a game. got to the end of barrockstadt, which is chapter 2, and i could not move on. absolutely awful port!
Syberia's VR adventure is really impressive. Too bad about the performance issues. Hopefully, they drop another 1 or 2 patches to fix it
No smooth turning, Had to resort to teleport movement, Iv finished the game so i wont be going back to it no matter what fixes you do, Oh and the games way to short.

Unfortunately, Syberia has some nasty bugs. For example, I repaired the bandstand in Baroque City, the violin automata are playing a song, but the professors won't give me any money. they still want me to repair it.

Not sure what’s wrong but it def has lag, and the frigging instructions, just…keep…popping…up and won’t move out of the way, totally keeping you from getting immersed?  The graphics are not very crisp, possibly something on my end, but so far got aggravated in 5 minutes with the same “helpful”hint popping up over and over and floating over the scene. Not sure I’ll keep it, maybe they will smooth it out, hope so it looks exciting to play
I love the game so far when I get stuck YouTube is a lot of help
I just love this series and I'm glad that it exists in VR now, I'd love another sequel to the series!
Renouveler une ancienne licence en VR... Challenge tenu

reviews.review_title: Good game held back by mobile hardware.
reviews.review_description: I get it, you have to get people to buy the headset but making us play the game on a headset as the only option is not really consumer friendly, especially when the hardware can barely run it. I have a 5090 desktop and a quest 3. Would have been great if Meta offered a PC version as well like Behemoth and others.
